title: Robust Yeasty Indian Potatoes Vada Pav
description: A yeasty and juicy Indian dish featuring sage, this egg-free friendly vada pav is a perfect snack for any adventurous food lover, made by dehydrating and then frying the potato mixture.
instructions: Boil the diced potatoes until they are tender, then mash and set aside.
In a pan, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat and saute the chopped onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger until the onion is translucent.
Add the chopped sage leaves, chili powder, and salt to the pan and cook for 1 minute.
In a large bowl, combine the mashed potatoes, sauteed onion mixture, and bread crumbs, and mix well.
Add the active dry yeast to the potato mixture and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 6-8 equal portions and shape each portion into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a patty and dehydrate for 30 minutes.
Heat the remaining oil in a deep frying pan over medium high heat and fry the patties until they are golden brown and crispy.
Serve the vada pav hot with your favorite chutney or sauce.
